purchase_triggers.trigger: Need to solve a specific problem (e.g., salary negotiation)
purchase_triggers.context: Seeking skill improvement

purchase_triggers.trigger: Genre affinity (e.g., romance, thriller)
purchase_triggers.context: Enjoys specific genre elements

purchase_triggers.trigger: Author reputation/expertise
purchase_triggers.context: Author is recognized as expert (e.g., teacher, physician)

purchase_triggers.trigger: ARC (Advance Reader Copy) offer
purchase_triggers.context: Received for honest review

objections.objection: Content is offensive/morally unacceptable
Lacks practical application/strategy
Inconsistent tone/world-building
Poor character development/unsympathetic characters
Too slow/predictable
Excessive slang or jargon disrupts flow
Not suitable for intended audience (e.g., too mature)

switching_triggers.reason: Lack of actionable, specific advice
switching_triggers.threshold: After first few chapters fail to deliver practical tips

switching_triggers.reason: Inconsistent tone, weak characters, slow pace
switching_triggers.threshold: When immersion breaks or boredom sets in

switching_triggers.reason: Moral/ethical discomfort
switching_triggers.threshold: Immediate upon encountering unacceptable material

deal_breakers: Complete lack of practical value/actionable insight

pain_points.signal: Lack of depth/exploration in content
Exhausting emotional rollercoasters in narratives
Stilted dialogue or uneven pacing
Unresolved plotlines or disappointing endings
Deceptive cover art misrepresenting content
Difficulty connecting with main characters

desired_outcomes.outcome: Thorough exploration of worlds/ideas
Realistic and resolved character relationships
Engaging, well-paced narratives
Feel-good, uplifting stories
Historical depth and authenticity
Emotional resonance and connection

purchase_triggers.trigger: Recommendation from trusted series/author
purchase_triggers.context: Addicted to author's other series, thrilled to try new one.

purchase_triggers.trigger: Discovery of free/inexpensive offer
purchase_triggers.context: Happened on this book as a Kindle free for a day and it turned out a win win.

purchase_triggers.trigger: Personal relevance to life experience
purchase_triggers.context: I have so much in common with the author. I have also been through a harrowing experience with the medical community.

purchase_triggers.trigger: Nostalgia or prior positive experience
purchase_triggers.context: Totally addicted to this series back in the 70's watching it on Masterpiece Theatre.

purchase_triggers.trigger: Strong, positive synopsis
purchase_triggers.context: I really wanted to read this book after scanning the synopsis.

objections.objection: Content too dark or emotionally draining
Plot lacks follow-through on interesting ideas
Unrealistic character behavior or excessive conflict
Deceptive or unappealing packaging (cover)
Ending is a cop-out or unsatisfying

switching_triggers.reason: Series became too dark and unenjoyable
switching_triggers.threshold: When novels had gotten so dark that I wasn't enjoying where the author was taking the characters.

pain_points.signal: Expectation mismatch: product does not match advertised genre (e.g., expecting mystery/thriller but getting literary fiction or police procedural)
Poor or excessive 'literary' writing that bores or annoys the reader
Slow pacing or bloated narrative with too much description
Unsatisfying or confusing endings with too many loose threads
Unlikable or unbelievable characters that are hard to connect with
Series books that don't work well as standalones, making them inaccessible
Feeling 'suckered in' by misleading claims or reviews
Difficulty seeing detail in book illustrations (e.g., dark printing in knitting patterns)

desired_outcomes.outcome: A well-written, fast-paced, engaging story that keeps the reader entertained
Clear, satisfying ending with all loose ends tied up
Believable, well-developed, and likable characters
Educational or informative content woven seamlessly into the narrative
Exotic or well-described settings that bring locations to life
Authentic, accurate background details (e.g., historical, legal, financial)
A book that works as a standalone, even if part of a series
Memorable, innovative designs or patterns (in craft books)
Characters who exhibit growth, redemption, or positive change
A 'breath of fresh air' with characters who avoid typical negative teen behaviors (e.g., drinking, drugs, excessive cursing)

purchase_triggers.trigger: Positive reputation of author or series (e.g., loyal fan, previous enjoyment)
purchase_triggers.context: Established series or author with proven track record

purchase_triggers.trigger: Promising book description matching preferred genre (e.g., mystery/thriller, page-turner)
purchase_triggers.context: Browsing or searching for specific genre

purchase_triggers.trigger: Recommendation or claim of being 'the new standard' or 'summer read' from trusted source
purchase_triggers.context: Influenced by reviews, blurbs, or media claims

purchase_triggers.trigger: Desire for educational or historical content presented in an engaging narrative
purchase_triggers.context: Seeking informative fiction or non-fiction

purchase_triggers.trigger: Need for specific skill-building (e.g., learning Spanish, knitting patterns)
purchase_triggers.context: Purposeful purchase for self-improvement or hobby

purchase_triggers.trigger: Emotional connection to themes (e.g., grief, redemption, community projects)
purchase_triggers.context: Personal resonance with book's subject matter

purchase_triggers.trigger: Availability on Kindle Unlimited or at a discounted price
purchase_triggers.context: Cost-conscious or subscription-based acquisition

objections.objection: Atrocious, overly descriptive, or boring writing style
Predictable plot or lack of suspense
Unsatisfying or confusing ending
Unlikable or poorly developed characters
Book doesn't work as a standalone (requires series knowledge)
Misleading advertising or genre mismatch
Excessive, bloated narrative with unnecessary detail
Unbelievable character actions or unrealistic elements (e.g., unhealthy diets, instant love)
Poor production quality (e.g., dark illustrations in craft books)

switching_triggers.from_product: Rosetta Stone or similar immersive language software
switching_triggers.reason: Preference for reading over listening to learn
switching_triggers.threshold: When supplemental material is needed to enhance learning style

switching_triggers.reason: Disappointment with recent installment (e.g., weaker plot, unlikable protagonist)
switching_triggers.threshold: When quality drops or character becomes unengaging

switching_triggers.from_product: Typical YA books with negative teen behaviors
switching_triggers.reason: Desire for cleaner, more positive content
switching_triggers.threshold: When seeking a 'breath of fresh air' in the genre

switching_triggers.reason: Preference for anecdotal, readable memoirs
switching_triggers.threshold: When content feels too dry or academic

decision_factors_ranked: Value for money or cost (discounts/Kindle Unlimited)
deal_breakers: Boring, overly 'literary' writing that puts reader to sleep
Misleading genre claims (e.g., expecting mystery but getting literary fiction)
Ending with too many loose threads or unsatisfying resolution
Excessive, unnecessary description that slows pace

pain_points.signal: Character development feels shallow or stereotypical
Story pacing is slow or plods in places
Unnecessary or overly graphic scenes that feel gratuitous
Frustration with weak, immature, or poorly written female leads
Predictable, safe, or superficial plotlines, especially in minority-focused stories
Romantic storylines or character relationships that feel flat, annoying, or unbelievable
Books being excessively long with filler content

desired_outcomes.outcome: Deeply developed, distinctive, and memorable characters
A well-plotted, engaging mystery or story that holds attention
Emotional connection and investment in the story and characters
Rich, immersive world-building and setting
A satisfying, heartwarming, or feel-good ending (HEA - Happily Ever After)
Escapism and entertainment
Learning about historical, social, or cultural issues
Humor, witty banter, and lighthearted fun

purchase_triggers.trigger: Author is a known favorite
purchase_triggers.context: Reviewer explicitly mentions being a fan of the author

purchase_triggers.trigger: Book is part of a beloved series
purchase_triggers.context: Reviewer follows a series and seeks the next installment

purchase_triggers.trigger: Positive buzz or recommendation
purchase_triggers.context: Book is described as important, a major debut, or highly recommended

purchase_triggers.trigger: Genre-specific appeal (e.g., Christmas story, romance tropes)
purchase_triggers.context: Reviewer seeks specific themes like holiday romances or fake-dating plots

purchase_triggers.trigger: Strong character chemistry and connection
purchase_triggers.context: Reviewer is drawn by descriptions of compelling character dynamics

purchase_triggers.trigger: Unique or captivating premise
purchase_triggers.context: Concept (e.g., externalized unconsciousness, historical angle) grabs attention

objections.objection: Graphic, excessive, or offensive violence/sexual assault scenes
Poorly executed romantic subplots or character relationships
Predictable, formulaic, or superficial storytelling
Inauthentic or clumsy handling of sensitive topics
Excessive length with unnecessary filler scenes
Frenetic, silly, or pointless dialogue/narrative style

switching_triggers.reason: Despise when female leads are written as weak and immature
switching_triggers.threshold: Immediate stop in reading if character is perceived as too passive

switching_triggers.reason: Scenes feel over-the-top and offensive, breaking suspension of disbelief
switching_triggers.threshold: Multiple instances of graphic content that feels unnecessary to the plot

deal_breakers: Gratuitous, offensive, or excessively graphic violence/sexual assault
Extremely weak, stereotypical, or poorly written protagonists
Clumsy, inauthentic handling of serious cultural or social issues
Romantic relationships that feel flat, annoying, or unbelievable

top_pain_points: Lack of time for cooking during weekdays
Difficulty finding specialty ingredients locally
Recipes not catering to dietary preferences (vegetarian/gluten-free)
Overly complex recipes with many steps/stages
Disappointment when cookbooks lack expected depth or coverage
High time and effort required to manage complexity and inefficiency
Lack of clarity and actionable guidance
Concern over unhealthy or non-organic ingredients
Difficulty finding suitable, practical solutions (e.g., gardening pests, cooking substitutions)
Budget constraints and desire for cost-effective, high-value solutions
Difficulty finding time for comprehensive preparation due to demanding schedules
Overwhelmed by complex concepts that require simplified, actionable guidance
top_desired_outcomes: Efficient, time-saving cooking solutions
Access to authentic, well-tested recipes with clear instructions
Recipes that accommodate dietary needs (vegetarian, gluten-free, etc.)
Learning new cooking techniques and cultural insights
High-quality results that impress or comfort
Ability to make ahead and freeze meals
Achieve clarity, actionable insights, and simplified strategies
Implement healthy, organic, and sustainable practices
Gain practical, time-efficient solutions
Find cost-effective and high-value products
Improve long-term well-being (health, financial, environmental)
Achieve high scores on critical exams (e.g., LSAT)
top_deal_breakers: Recipes predominantly meat-based with few vegetarian options
Instructions that are confusing or lack step-by-step guidance
Key content omissions or superficial coverage
Uses unhealthy, non-organic, or inflammatory ingredients (e.g., agave, vegetable oil)
Lacks clear, actionable, step-by-step guidance
Overly complex, confusing, or theoretical without practical application
High cost without corresponding high value or proven results
Fails to address specific pain points (e.g., gophers, healthy substitutions)
Lack of step-by-step guidance
Insufficient depth for advanced users
top_purchase_triggers: Recommendation from trusted sources (blogs, authors, awards)
Focus on specific dietary or lifestyle needs (vegetarian, gluten-free)
Clear, practical organization and helpful extras (metric conversions, tips)
Nostalgia or connection to cultural/heritage foods
Positive previous experience with author or series
Evidence of comprehensive, actionable content
Strong visual appeal and practical examples
Credible author background and proven expertise
